description: Shared registry of check types used by all component audit-rules.json files. Each rule in a component file must reference a type defined here. This ensures the audit engine has a consistent, extensible vocabulary across all components.

rule_types.description: Checks that a required ARIA attribute is present on a target element, with an acceptable value. Fails if the attribute is missing or empty.

expected_input.selector: CSS selector string targeting the element to check
expected_input.valid_values: Array of acceptable string values, or null if any non-empty value is acceptable
expected_input.condition: Optional — check applies only when this secondary selector also matches the element

rule_types.description: Checks that a state-communicating attribute (aria-pressed, aria-expanded, aria-selected, aria-checked) is present on elements that implement a stateful interaction pattern. Value must be 'true' or 'false' — not undefined.

expected_input.selector: CSS selector for the interactive element
expected_input.trigger: What makes this check apply (e.g. data-toggle attribute, class name, or explicit annotation)

rule_types.description: Checks that a visible focus ring is present when the element receives keyboard focus. Fails if :focus-visible has outline:none without an equivalent replacement (border, box-shadow). This is a CSS-computed check, not just a source-file check.

expected_input.selector: CSS selector for the focusable element
expected_input.forbidden_css: CSS property-value pairs that suppress focus visibility
expected_input.acceptable_alternatives: List of CSS properties that constitute a valid replacement focus indicator

rule_types.description: Checks that the correct disabled state implementation is used. Distinguishes between HTML disabled (removes from tab order) and aria-disabled (keeps in tab order). Fails on contradictory combinations such as aria-disabled='true' + tabindex='-1'.

expected_input.selector: CSS selector for the button element
expected_input.allowed_patterns: List of valid attribute combinations for the disabled state

rule_types.description: Checks that a specific CSS class is applied only to semantically appropriate HTML elements. Non-interactive elements (div, span) may only carry the class if they also have role and tabindex attributes that restore interactivity.

expected_input.allowed_tags: Array of allowed HTML tag names
expected_input.fallback_requirements: Attributes required when tag is not in allowed_tags (e.g. role=button, tabindex)

rule_types.description: Checks that certain HTML elements do not appear as descendants of a target element. Used to enforce HTML content model rules (e.g. no interactive content inside button) and design system structural rules.

expected_input.selector: CSS selector for the parent element
expected_input.forbidden_descendants: Array of tag names or CSS selectors that must not appear within the parent

rule_types.description: Checks that CSS properties on a selector do not use hardcoded values. For color properties, values must reference a CSS custom property (var(--token-name)). For directional properties, physical values (padding-left, margin-right) must not be present at all when logical equivalents should be used.

expected_input.properties: Array of CSS property names to check
expected_input.allowed_literal_values: Values exempt from the check (e.g. transparent, none, inherit, currentColor)
expected_input.forbidden_properties: Optional — property names that must not appear at all (for RTL checks)

rule_types.description: Checks that specific CSS property names are absent from a target selector. Used to prohibit physical-direction properties (padding-left, margin-right, border-left) in favour of their logical equivalents (padding-inline-start, margin-inline-end, border-inline-start). Distinct from no_hardcoded_values: this type checks that a property name does not appear at all, regardless of its value.

expected_input.forbidden_properties: Array of CSS property names that must not be present on the selector
expected_input.logical_alternatives: Optional — array of recommended logical-property replacements, for use in error messages and auto-fix tooling

rule_types.description: Checks that the text content of a target element falls within defined minimum and maximum character bounds. Used to enforce label length rules that prevent text from overflowing fixed-height containers.

expected_input.selector: CSS selector for the text-bearing element

rule_types.description: Checks that a named CSS custom property is defined in the token stylesheet. Used to verify that the token surface documented in the component contract is actually declared in token.css.

expected_input.source_file: Path to the token file relative to design-system root

rule_types.description: Checks that a specific CSS custom property is referenced (via var()) in the component's stylesheet. Confirms that the component is actually consuming a token from the token surface, not a hardcoded fallback.
Checks that no more than N elements matching a selector appear within the same ancestor scope. Used to enforce visual hierarchy rules such as 'at most one primary button per visual group'.

expected_input.selector: CSS selector for the elements to count
expected_input.scope: Ancestor selector that defines the boundary of the count
expected_input.max_count: Maximum allowed occurrences within the scope

rule_types.description: Checks that an interactive element is operable via keyboard without a mouse. For native elements (button, a) this is implicit. For custom elements (div[role=button]), verifies tabindex and that a keydown/keyup handler is attached for Enter and Space.

expected_input.selector: CSS selector for the interactive element
expected_input.required_keys: Array of key names that must trigger the primary action
expected_input.native_elements_exempt: Boolean — if true, native button/a elements pass without JS check
